ip_outcome_note: Depicted small round creature with silver hex-nut head and liquid metal body matches Meltan's canonical design exactly. National Pokedex no. 808. Mythical Steel-type. Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-129).

trivia_bullets.text: Meltan (National Pokedex no. 808) is a Mythical Steel-type introduced in 2018 as the first Pokemon to debut in Pokemon GO before any mainline series game. Series director Junichi Masuda worked with Niantic to introduce Meltan as a cross-platform bridge, revealed during the September 22, 2018 Community Day in a deliberate substitution surprise. Its evolution Melmetal can only be obtained in Let's Go, Pikachu/Eevee by transferring from GO — one of the franchise's most explicit cross-game dependency designs. [WebSearch snippet: Wikipedia Meltan; WebSearch snippet: pokemonletsgo.pokemon.com]

Flavor text: "It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy." — the hex-nut head is the one solid feature; the body is liquid, fed entirely by consuming and metabolizing iron and steel. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-129]

Meltan's appearance in Darkness Ablaze is notable because it is a Mythical Pokemon on a Common-rarity card (no.129/189). Mythical Pokemon in the TCG are typically distributed as promotional cards or restricted prints; a common-rarity Mythical in a standard set expansion is rare. The card's low attack values (Headbutt 10, Beam 20) reflect its status as a setup piece for Melmetal rather than a standalone threat. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-129]
